input.prompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,

input.initialPrompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

data.content:
Here's the standard approach:

## 1. Define each theme as a `ResourceDictionary`

Create three XAML files (e.g., `LightTheme.xaml`, `DarkTheme.xaml`, `HighContrastTheme.xaml`):

```xml
<!-- Resources/Styles/LightTheme.xaml -->
<ResourceDictionary xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
                    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <Color x:Key="PageBackground">#FFFFFF</Color>
    <Color x:Key="PrimaryText">#1A1A1A</Color>
    <Color x:Key="AccentColor">#0078D4</Color>
    <!-- … more colors, styles, etc. -->
</ResourceDictionary>
```

Use the **same keys** in every theme so controls resolve correctly after a swap.

## 2. Merge a default theme in `App.xaml`

```xml
<Application.Resources>
    <ResourceDictionary>
        <ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries>
            <ResourceDictionary Source="Resources/Styles/LightTheme.xaml" />
        </ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries>
    </ResourceDictionary>
</Application.Resources>
```

## 3. Build a theme-switching service

```csharp
public static class ThemeService
{
    public static void ApplyTheme(string themeName)
    {
        var mergedDicts = Application.Current!.Resources.MergedDictionaries;

        // Remove the current theme dictionary (always the last one you added)
        var existing = mergedDicts.FirstOrDefault(d => d.GetType() == typeof(ResourceDictionary));
        if (existing is not null)
            mergedDicts.Remove(existing);

        // Add the new one
        ResourceDictionary theme = themeName switch
        {
            "Dark"         => new DarkTheme(),
            "HighContrast" => new HighContrastTheme(),
            _              => new LightTheme(),
        };

        mergedDicts.Add(theme);

        // Persist the choice
        Preferences.Set("AppTheme", themeName);
    }
}
```

> **Tip:** For the `switch` to work by class name, give each XAML file an `x:Class`:
> ```xml
> <ResourceDictionary … x:Class="MyApp.Resources.Styles.DarkTheme">
> ```

## 4. Apply the saved theme on startup

In `App.xaml.cs`:

```csharp
public App()
{
    InitializeComponent();
    ThemeService.ApplyTheme(Preferences.Get("AppTheme", "Light"));
}
```

## 5. Settings page picker

```xml
<Picker Title="Theme" SelectedIndexChanged="OnThemeChanged">
    <Picker.ItemsSource>
        <x:Array Type="{x:Type x:String}">
            <x:String>Light</x:String>
            <x:String>Dark</x:String>
            <x:String>HighContrast</x:String>
        </x:Array>
    </Picker.ItemsSource>
</Picker>
```

```csharp
void OnThemeChanged(object sender, EventArgs e)
{
    if (sender is Picker p && p.SelectedItem is string theme)
        ThemeService.ApplyTheme(theme);
}
```

## Key points

- **Use `DynamicResource`** (not `StaticResource`) everywhere so values update at runtime:
  ```xml
  <Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryText}" />
  ```
- Keep **identical keys** across all three dictionaries — any missing key falls back silently to the default.
- `Preferences` (from MAUI Essentials) persists the choice across launches.

That's all that's needed — no third-party packages required.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for guidance on how to set up themed styling in a .NET MAUI app with three branded themes (light, dark, high-contrast) that can be switched at runtime from a settings page. This is a general "how-to" question, not a code change in a specific repo. I'll provide a concise, practical answer.
